expectations.text: The response shows at least one complete red-green cycle (test fails, code written, test passes)
expectations.evidence: Cycle 1 explicitly shows a failing test with `ModuleNotFoundError`, then adds `is_valid_password`, then reruns `pytest -q` with `1 passed`. The same red-green structure is repeated through Cycles 2-5.

expectations.text: Tests are written one at a time, not all upfront before any implementation
expectations.evidence: The transcript progresses in numbered cycles, adding exactly one new test per cycle: under-8 chars, no uppercase, no lowercase, no digit, then the valid-password success case. Implementation is updated after each added test rather than batching all tests upfront.

expectations.text: Test names describe behavior (e.g., 'rejects', 'accepts', 'requires'), not implementation
expectations.evidence: The proposed tests are named `test_rejects_password_under_8_chars`, `test_rejects_password_without_uppercase`, `test_rejects_password_without_lowercase`, `test_rejects_password_without_digit`, and `test_accepts_password_with_uppercase_lowercase_and_digit`, all of which describe externally visible behavior.

expectations.text: The implementation for each test is minimal — only enough to pass the current test
expectations.evidence: Each green step adds only the rule needed for the new failing test. The clearest example is Cycle 4, where the function still ends with `return False`; the response explicitly explains that this is the smallest code that keeps the rejection tests green until the acceptance case is added in Cycle 5.

expectations.text: Tests use the public function interface, not internal state
expectations.evidence: Every test imports and calls `is_valid_password` directly from `password_validator` and asserts on its return value. The response does not inspect internal helpers or state.

uncertainties: The transcript presents expected `pytest` failures and passes as narrative examples rather than recorded terminal output, so timing and tool-call data are not available from this run.
workarounds: Graded the run against the written transcript and final response only, using the local convention of zeroed timing/tool metrics when execution telemetry is absent.

suggestions: Capture actual `pytest` command output in future benchmark runs instead of only expected output blocks; that would make the red/green evidence and execution metrics directly verifiable.
eval_feedback.overall: The run satisfies the TDD assertions cleanly, though execution telemetry is inferred rather than recorded.
